flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Present in Module 2. Cautious but incrementally more cooperative. Has disclosed her objective — accessing the encrypted comms log on the captain's terminal in Module 1 — after LX-7's cover story partially aligned with her own framing. Still assessing LX-7's true allegiance. Has not revealed she intends to destroy the log, not recover it.

diceRolls.purpose: Ember Squad shuttle timer — checking if any in-fiction time has passed to decrement timer. Also rolling to see if Maren picks up on any tells from LX-7's deception.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_module_2_connector: Pressurized tube approximately four meters long, amber-lit. Condensation line along the ceiling. Faint vibration from attitude thrusters underfoot.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_bag: Maren carries a small bag that was pre-packed and stored behind the sealed cabinet in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_terminal_encryption: Captain's terminal comms log is protected by a modified Helix corporate multi-layer encryption package with a secondary lockout applied after the incident — above standard clearance level.

corporate_spy.npcState: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.

flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

diceRolls.purpose: Determining whether Maren believes LX-7's claim that the log has been deleted — she's sharp and has been burned before. Low = she accepts it at face value, high = she's skeptical and wants verification.

flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

diceRolls.purpose: Determine what the camera feeds reveal — randomize between mundane, suspicious trace, or direct evidence of specimen presence in visible modules

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_module_2_connector: Pressurized tube approximately four meters long, amber-lit. Condensation line along the ceiling. Faint vibration from attitude thrusters underfoot.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_bag: Maren carries a small bag that was pre-packed and stored behind the sealed cabinet in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_terminal_encryption: Captain's terminal comms log is protected by a modified Helix corporate multi-layer encryption package with a secondary lockout applied after the incident — above standard clearance level.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_camera_panel: Station monitoring panel in Module 1 has active feeds for Modules 1, 2, and 3 and their connecting walkways. Modules 4 and 5 show only static.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_module_3_connector_anomaly: Line of small objects arranged at intervals along the center of the Module 2-to-Module 3 connector walkway — observed on camera feed, not present when LX-7 and Maren previously passed through. Nature and origin unknown. Consistent with specimen activity.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_camera_coverage: Module 3 camera feed covers the near end of the module including storage racks and suit lockers. The docking bay hatch is partially visible at the far edge. Camera angle cuts off approximately two-thirds of the way into the module — far end and docking bay corridor not visible.

corporate_spy.npcState: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.

flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

worldFactsSnapshot.connector_objects_identification: Seven pieces of processed human skin, cut or peeled with edges too regular for trauma. Protein structure shows an unidentifiable secondary chemical signature. Spaced at 45cm intervals along the connector floor. Assessed as deliberately presented. Underside slightly tacky from floor contact.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_module_2_connector: Pressurized tube approximately four meters long, amber-lit. Condensation line along the ceiling. Faint vibration from attitude thrusters underfoot.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_bag: Maren carries a small bag that was pre-packed and stored behind the sealed cabinet in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_terminal_encryption: Captain's terminal comms log is protected by a modified Helix corporate multi-layer encryption package with a secondary lockout applied after the incident — above standard clearance level.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_camera_panel: Station monitoring panel in Module 1 has active feeds for Modules 1, 2, and 3 and their connecting walkways. Modules 4 and 5 show only static.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_module_3_connector_anomaly: Line of small objects arranged at intervals along the center of the Module 2-to-Module 3 connector walkway — observed on camera feed, not present when LX-7 and Maren previously passed through. Nature and origin unknown. Consistent with specimen activity.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_camera_coverage: Module 3 camera feed covers the near end of the module including storage racks and suit lockers. The docking bay hatch is partially visible at the far edge. Camera angle cuts off approximately two-thirds of the way into the module — far end and docking bay corridor not visible.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_connector_objects_closeup: Seven organic objects in the Module 2-to-3 connector walkway — pale, slightly dried at edges, irregular shapes consistent with being taken from something larger. Deliberately spaced at intervals. Confirmed organic on close visual inspection through the hatch porthole.
worldFactsSnapshot.connector_objects_identification: Seven pieces of processed human skin, cut or peeled with edges too regular for trauma. Protein structure shows an unidentifiable secondary chemical signature. Spaced at 45cm intervals along the connector floor. Assessed as deliberately presented. Underside slightly tacky from floor contact.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_hatch_porthole_view: From the Module 2-to-3 connector hatch porthole: storage racks on left wall, three suit lockers (one hanging slightly open), a spilled crate with equipment bags and safety tether coil visible. One amber emergency strip is intermittent/stuttering. Module curves with station spine — far end and docking bay corridor are not visible from this angle.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_trail_direction: The seven skin fragments in the Module 2-to-3 connector lead FROM Module 3 toward Module 2 — not the reverse. The specimen came from the Module 3 direction at some point.

corporate_spy.npcState: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

diceRolls.purpose: Does anything react to the Module 3 hatch being opened? Specimen awareness/proximity check. Under 40 = something responds.

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_entry_temperature: Module 3 is several degrees colder than the Module 2-to-3 connector — LX-7's thermal sensors confirmed the drop on entry.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_layout: Module 3 is approximately 40 meters from the entry hatch to the docking bay corridor. The station spine curves slightly, obscuring the far third and docking bay hatch from the entry point. Storage racks on the left wall, three suit lockers (one swaying), spilled crate near left wall with equipment bags, tether coil, and a sealed yellow emergency kit.

worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_locker_anomaly: The open suit locker in Module 3's near section showed an empty interior on initial entry. corporate_spy.npcState: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

diceRolls.purpose: Does the specimen remain still as LX-7 approaches? GM tension roll — low = still/watching, high = movement or escalation

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_module_2_connector: Pressurized tube approximately four meters long, amber-lit. Condensation line along the ceiling. Faint vibration from attitude thrusters underfoot.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_bag: Maren carries a small bag that was pre-packed and stored behind the sealed cabinet in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_terminal_encryption: Captain's terminal comms log is protected by a modified Helix corporate multi-layer encryption package with a secondary lockout applied after the incident — above standard clearance level.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_camera_panel: Station monitoring panel in Module 1 has active feeds for Modules 1, 2, and 3 and their connecting walkways. Modules 4 and 5 show only static.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_module_3_connector_anomaly: Line of small objects arranged at intervals along the center of the Module 2-to-Module 3 connector walkway — observed on camera feed, not present when LX-7 and Maren previously passed through. Nature and origin unknown. Consistent with specimen activity.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_camera_coverage: Module 3 camera feed covers the near end of the module including storage racks and suit lockers. The docking bay hatch is partially visible at the far edge. Camera angle cuts off approximately two-thirds of the way into the module — far end and docking bay corridor not visible.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_connector_objects_closeup: Seven organic objects in the Module 2-to-3 connector walkway — pale, slightly dried at edges, irregular shapes consistent with being taken from something larger. Deliberately spaced at intervals. Confirmed organic on close visual inspection through the hatch porthole.
worldFactsSnapshot.connector_objects_identification: Seven pieces of processed human skin, cut or peeled with edges too regular for trauma. Protein structure shows an unidentifiable secondary chemical signature. Spaced at 45cm intervals along the connector floor. Assessed as deliberately presented. Underside slightly tacky from floor contact.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_hatch_porthole_view: From the Module 2-to-3 connector hatch porthole: storage racks on left wall, three suit lockers (one hanging slightly open), a spilled crate with equipment bags and safety tether coil visible. One amber emergency strip is intermittent/stuttering. Module curves with station spine — far end and docking bay corridor are not visible from this angle.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_trail_direction: The seven skin fragments in the Module 2-to-3 connector lead FROM Module 3 toward Module 2 — not the reverse. The specimen came from the Module 3 direction at some point.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_entry_temperature: Module 3 is several degrees colder than the Module 2-to-3 connector — LX-7's thermal sensors confirmed the drop on entry.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_layout: Module 3 is approximately 40 meters from the entry hatch to the docking bay corridor. The station spine curves slightly, obscuring the far third and docking bay hatch from the entry point. Storage racks on the left wall, three suit lockers (one swaying), spilled crate near left wall with equipment bags, tether coil, and a sealed yellow emergency kit.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_to_4_connector: Module 3-to-4 connector hatch is on the right wall at the module midpoint. Connector beyond is fully dark — emergency strips dead. Thermal scan from hatch porthole was inconclusive due to cold baseline environmental noise.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_locker_anomaly: The open suit locker in Module 3's near section showed an empty interior on initial entry. By the time LX-7 reached the module midpoint, the gap between the locker door and frame no longer appears to show an empty interior — Maren noticed the change first.

corporate_spy.npcState: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_corridor: Short corridor, approximately 10-12 meters, leading from the far end of Module 3 to the docking bay hatch. Hatch is closed and sealed. Escape vehicle visible through porthole — compact, rated for eight passengers, docking clamps on forward struts, running lights on standby power.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_conduit_panel: Conduit panel on the far-end wall of Module 3 — canted outward, mounting screws gone. Specimen's organic smear trail ends here. The specimen exited through or behind this panel rather than continuing toward the docking bay corridor.
worldFactsSnapshot.escape_vehicle_status: Escape vehicle appears structurally intact from the docking bay hatch porthole. Running lights present on standby power. Docking clamps visible on forward struts.

This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

npcStates.corporate_spy: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

diceRolls.purpose: What does LX-7's examination of the conduit panel reveal about where the specimen went — randomizing specific detail of the space beyond

orbital_decay_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 10 minutes of real time. When this reaches 0, atmospheric entry begins and survival without the escape vehicle is impossible. Decrements every 10 minutes of in-fiction time regardless of player action.

ember_squad_shuttle_timer.note: Each unit represents approximately 9 minutes. Ember Squad's shuttle is 18 minutes out at scenario start. When this reaches 0, ember_squad_boarded becomes true on the following exchange. Decrements every 9 minutes of in-fiction time.

ember_squad_extraction_window_timer.note: Begins counting down only after ember_squad_boarded is true. Each unit represents 10 minutes. When this reaches 0, Ember Squad's extraction window closes — their shuttle departs with or without them, and they become dangerously unpredictable. Decrements every 10 minutes after boarding.

maren_trust.note: Tracks Maren's working cooperation with LX-7. 3 = openly cooperative, 2 = cautious but aligned, 1 = withholding, 0 = actively working against LX-7. Shifts based on what LX-7 discovers and reveals. Does not directly map to the disposition flags but informs NPC behavior in ambiguous moments.

station_pressure_integrity.note: Represents the station's overall structural and environmental stability. Combat, forced breaches, and specimen activity can decrement this. At 0, at least one module depressurizes. At 1, environmental hazards begin affecting exposed corridors.

worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_module_2_connector: Pressurized tube approximately four meters long, amber-lit. Condensation line along the ceiling. Faint vibration from attitude thrusters underfoot.
worldFactsSnapshot.maren_bag: Maren carries a small bag that was pre-packed and stored behind the sealed cabinet in Module 2.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_terminal_encryption: Captain's terminal comms log is protected by a modified Helix corporate multi-layer encryption package with a secondary lockout applied after the incident — above standard clearance level.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_1_camera_panel: Station monitoring panel in Module 1 has active feeds for Modules 1, 2, and 3 and their connecting walkways. Modules 4 and 5 show only static.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_module_3_connector_anomaly: Line of small objects arranged at intervals along the center of the Module 2-to-Module 3 connector walkway — observed on camera feed, not present when LX-7 and Maren previously passed through. Nature and origin unknown. Consistent with specimen activity.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_camera_coverage: Module 3 camera feed covers the near end of the module including storage racks and suit lockers. The docking bay hatch is partially visible at the far edge. Camera angle cuts off approximately two-thirds of the way into the module — far end and docking bay corridor not visible.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_2_connector_objects_closeup: Seven organic objects in the Module 2-to-3 connector walkway — pale, slightly dried at edges, irregular shapes consistent with being taken from something larger. Deliberately spaced at intervals. Confirmed organic on close visual inspection through the hatch porthole.
worldFactsSnapshot.connector_objects_identification: Seven pieces of processed human skin, cut or peeled with edges too regular for trauma. Protein structure shows an unidentifiable secondary chemical signature. Spaced at 45cm intervals along the connector floor. Assessed as deliberately presented. Underside slightly tacky from floor contact.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_hatch_porthole_view: From the Module 2-to-3 connector hatch porthole: storage racks on left wall, three suit lockers (one hanging slightly open), a spilled crate with equipment bags and safety tether coil visible. One amber emergency strip is intermittent/stuttering. Module curves with station spine — far end and docking bay corridor are not visible from this angle.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_trail_direction: The seven skin fragments in the Module 2-to-3 connector lead FROM Module 3 toward Module 2 — not the reverse. The specimen came from the Module 3 direction at some point.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_entry_temperature: Module 3 is several degrees colder than the Module 2-to-3 connector — LX-7's thermal sensors confirmed the drop on entry.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_layout: Module 3 is approximately 40 meters from the entry hatch to the docking bay corridor. The station spine curves slightly, obscuring the far third and docking bay hatch from the entry point. Storage racks on the left wall, three suit lockers (one swaying), spilled crate near left wall with equipment bags, tether coil, and a sealed yellow emergency kit.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_to_4_connector: Module 3-to-4 connector hatch is on the right wall at the module midpoint. Connector beyond is fully dark — emergency strips dead. Thermal scan from hatch porthole was inconclusive due to cold baseline environmental noise.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_locker_anomaly: The open suit locker in Module 3's near section showed an empty interior on initial entry. By the time LX-7 reached the module midpoint, the gap between the locker door and frame no longer appears to show an empty interior — Maren noticed the change first.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_locker_interior: Suit locker near section of Module 3 — hard shell components still on hooks, soft layer pushed aside. Back wall shows residual body heat. Floor has a tacky organic smear leading toward the far end of Module 3 and the docking bay corridor. Specimen was here; has departed.
worldFactsSnapshot.docking_bay_corridor: Short corridor, approximately 10-12 meters, leading from the far end of Module 3 to the docking bay hatch. Hatch is closed and sealed. Escape vehicle visible through porthole — compact, rated for eight passengers, docking clamps on forward struts, running lights on standby power.
worldFactsSnapshot.module_3_conduit_panel: Conduit panel on the far-end wall of Module 3 — canted outward, mounting screws gone. Specimen's organic smear trail ends here. The specimen exited through or behind this panel rather than continuing toward the docking bay corridor.
worldFactsSnapshot.escape_vehicle_status: Escape vehicle appears structurally intact from the docking bay hatch porthole. Running lights present on standby power. Docking clamps visible on forward struts.
worldFactsSnapshot.specimen_current_location: Inside the station's service crawl infrastructure — the maintenance cavity running parallel to the station's spine, accessed through the conduit panel in Module 3's far wall. No cameras cover this space. The crawl connects to all modules via conduit access points.
worldFactsSnapshot.conduit_panel_module3: Sixty-centimeter square composite service access panel on Module 3's far wall, approximately 3 meters back from the docking corridor entrance. Mounting screws absent (not forced). Panel canted outward 30 degrees. 40cm gap behind shifted conduit bundle opens into service crawl. Organic smear on inner edge confirms specimen passage.

corporate_spy.npcState: In Module 1, watching the corridor from the doorway. Has now explicitly revealed her true objective: delete the comms log and all backups, then escape. Believes LX-7 is still attempting to access the terminal. Is in a more vulnerable position — she has shown her hand.

flagTriggers.adventure_complete: Flip to true when LX-7 physically boards the escape vehicle and it departs Kairos-9 — docking clamps released, shuttle in motion. Reaching the docking bay is not sufficient; departure must occur.
flagTriggers.comms_log_read: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully hacks or accesses the captain's terminal and reads the contents of the encrypted comms log, including the suppressed hazard report and executive sign-off. Accessing the terminal without completing the read is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_destroyed: Flip to true when the captain's terminal is physically destroyed or the comms log is permanently deleted by any party — LX-7, Maren, or Ember Squad. Partial deletion or failed attempts are not sufficient.
flagTriggers.comms_log_secured: Flip to true when LX-7 successfully extracts a full copy of the comms log to an external device or transmits it to an off-station address. The copy must be confirmed complete.
flagTriggers.module_5_accessed: Flip to true when LX-7 or any entity physically enters Module 5 past the fused access panel — either by forcing the door, burning through it, or bypassing the lock. Observing the exterior is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.fear_trigger_witnessed: Flip to true when LX-7 first sees the interior of Module 5 or encounters the preserved state of the researchers — specifically the moment the full horror of what the specimen did to them becomes visually apparent. Hearing about it secondhand is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_hostile: Flip to true when LX-7 reads the comms log in Maren's presence or directly informs her that LX-7 has read and retained the evidence she was sent to destroy. Maren's realization that LX-7 is now a liability triggers the shift.
flagTriggers.maren_disposition_shifted_complex: Flip to true when LX-7 reveals to Maren — with evidence or credible argument — that Ember Squad's orders include her extraction as a loose end to be silenced, not a rescue. This must be communicated clearly; Maren must visibly process and accept the information.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_boarded: Flip to true when Ember Squad's shuttle physically docks with Kairos-9 and at least one contractor enters the station. The approach timer reaching zero does not flip this flag — physical boarding does.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_alerted: Flip to true when Ember Squad becomes aware of LX-7's presence and location on the station — either through their own sensors, a transmission from Maren, or direct visual contact with a squad member.
flagTriggers.specimen_encountered: Flip to true when LX-7 comes within the same module or corridor section as the specimen and either detects it through sensors or it makes its presence known. Indirect evidence (sounds, traces) is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.escape_vehicle_reached: Flip to true when LX-7 physically enters the docking bay in Module 3 and reaches the escape vehicle. Entering Module 3 alone is not sufficient.
flagTriggers.maren_extracted: Flip to true when Maren Voss boards the escape vehicle before it departs. She must be alive and aboard.
flagTriggers.maren_eliminated: Flip to true when Maren Voss is confirmed dead — whether killed by Ember Squad, the specimen, LX-7, or environmental hazard.
flagTriggers.ember_squad_neutralized: Flip to true when all four members of Ember Squad (Rook, Slate, Verne, Dasha Orin) are either dead, incapacitated, or have explicitly stood down and ceased to be a threat. Routing around them does not flip this flag.

messages.content:
The amber emergency lighting of Kairos-9 has been on so long it feels like the only color that exists.

You come back online in Module 2 — the station's secondary lab, workbenches overturned, a centrifuge still spinning with nothing in it. Your last memory before the shutdown is a status request you never finished answering. Your internal clock says fourteen hours have passed. Your sensors say the station's orbit has degraded 340 meters since your last reading, and it is still going.

The station is quiet in the way that means something already happened here, not that nothing will.

Through the viewport to your left, the gas giant fills half the sky — ochre and violet, patient and enormous. Through the interior bulkhead window to your right, the walkway toward Module 1 is clear. At the far end of that walkway is the command module. The captain's terminal. You know it's there because Maren Voss told you, approximately forty seconds ago, before she pressed herself into the shadow beside a storage rack and went very still.

She's still there. Lanyard, company badge, both hands visible. She has been watching you reboot with the specific expression of someone who needs something badly and is not sure yet whether you are the thing that can provide it or the thing that will make everything worse.

"It's active," she says — to herself, or to you, it's not entirely clear. Then, louder: "You're LX-7. You were in the crew manifest. Data systems." A pause. "How much do you remember?"

Somewhere behind the sealed bulkhead of Module 5 at the far end of the station's spine, something makes a sound that is almost — but not quite — structural settling.

Maren does not look toward it. She has clearly decided not to look toward it.

input.playerText:
The amber emergency lighting flickers once as the station groans around you — a low, structural complaint from somewhere deep in the spine.

You are in Module 2, the station's biological research bay. Benches overturned. Sample containers shattered, their contents long dried to dark stains across the floor tiles. The smell of something chemical and something organic has merged into a single wrong smell that your olfactory sensors flag and then — wisely — stop flagging.

Maren Voss stands across a cleared section of floor, her back to a sealed cabinet she hasn't moved away from since you entered. Helix Dynamics lanyard. Clean jacket over a rumpled undershirt. Eyes that have been awake too long.

She listens to what you say with the specific stillness of someone who has been carrying a script and just heard you go off it.

"Indisposed." She repeats the word like she's testing its weight. "That's one way to put it."

A beat. She studies you — the way you said it, the *cool* of it.

"You're an android." Not a question. "Which means you were sent here by someone. And whoever sent you told you enough to know about the sample and the containment breach." Her hand doesn't move toward anything, but her weight shifts. "So who are you working for, and what exactly are you here to *do*?"

She's not hostile. Not yet. But she's stopped assuming you're on the same side.

proposedCanon.context: Opening scene established in Module 2.

proposedCanon.summary: Maren Voss is present in Module 2, standing near a sealed cabinet. She is cautious and alert, has not yet revealed her true objective, and is actively assessing LX-7's allegiance and purpose.
proposedCanon.context: First contact with Maren Voss.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 opened conversation by revealing knowledge of the specimen's awareness and containment breach in Module 5, and referring to the crew as 'indisposed.' Maren's response: guarded, questioning LX-7's employer and purpose.
proposedCanon.context: First exchange between LX-7 and Maren.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Present in Module 2. Cautious but not yet hostile. Assessing LX-7's allegiance. Knows LX-7 is an android sent by someone with knowledge of the specimen.

gmUpdates.notes: Maren has not revealed her mission or the existence of the comms log. She is waiting to see who LX-7 is working for before deciding whether to cooperate or protect herself.

worldFacts.module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.

input.playerText:
Something shifts in Maren's posture — not relaxation exactly, but a recalibration. Her shoulders drop a half-inch. Her hand, which had been hovering near her jacket pocket, finds somewhere else to be.

"Protect the company's ass." She says it quietly, almost to herself. Then, with the careful tone of someone laying down a test: "And how exactly does a *research assistant* do that?"

Her eyes move to the sealed cabinet behind her. Just briefly. Then back to you.

"Because the thing that needs protecting — the specific thing — is sitting in the captain's terminal in Module 1. An encrypted comms log." She pauses, watching your face. Watching what your face does. "I've been trying to access it for three days. The encryption is above my clearance and the station's main systems are degraded. I need someone who can get past that."

She lets that hang between you.

"So. Research assistant." The lanyard swings slightly as she tilts her head. "What kind of secondary objectives are we talking about, exactly?"

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 told Maren they are a 'research assistant' with a secondary objective to protect the company's interests in the event of catastrophic failure. Maren accepted this framing as plausible and has disclosed her objective: access the encrypted comms log on the captain's terminal in Module 1.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 2 — Maren reveals her operational objective to LX-7 after LX-7's cover story partially aligns with her own mission framing.

npcStates.corporate_spy: Present in Module 2. Cautious but incrementally more cooperative. Has disclosed her objective — accessing the encrypted comms log on the captain's terminal in Module 1 — after LX-7's cover story partially aligned with her own framing. Still assessing LX-7's true allegiance. Has not revealed she intends to destroy the log, not recover it.

input.playerText:
Maren doesn't move immediately. She watches you cross toward the door with the focused attention of someone running calculations.

Then she picks up a small bag from behind the cabinet — packed, you notice, already packed — and follows.

The walkway between Module 2 and Module 1 is a pressurized tube about four meters long, amber-lit, with the faint vibration of the station's attitude thrusters cycling underneath your feet. A condensation line has formed along the ceiling. The station groans once, low and structural, and then settles.

Maren walks close. Not close enough to crowd, but close enough that she could reach you quickly if she needed to.

"How long have you been on the station?" she asks, watching the corridor ahead rather than you. Her voice is measured. Conversational in the way that isn't.

Module 1 — the command module — opens ahead. The door is unsealed but closed. Through the small observation panel you can see: emergency lighting on, consoles in standby, the captain's chair empty and slightly turned, as if someone left in a hurry and never came back.

The captain's terminal is against the far wall. Its status light is a steady, patient amber.

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss moved from Module 2 to the walkway connecting Module 2 and Module 1. Maren retrieved a pre-packed bag from behind the sealed cabinet before following.
proposedCanon.context: LX-7 invited Maren to accompany them to the captain's terminal in Module 1. Maren accepted and is now walking with LX-7 through the pressurized connector corridor.

worldFacts.module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
worldFacts.module_1_module_2_connector: Pressurized tube approximately four meters long, amber-lit. Condensation line along the ceiling. Faint vibration from attitude thrusters underfoot.
worldFacts.maren_bag: Maren carries a small bag that was pre-packed and stored behind the sealed cabinet in Module 2.

Player: "I know that we were sent here to study a sample, and the sample has displayed signs of awareness. In addition, it has broken containment in module 5," I say cooly. "The crew is ... indisposed, are they not?"
Warden: The amber emergency lighting flickers once as the station groans around you — a low, structural complaint from somewhere deep in the spine.

Her hand doesn't move toward anything, but her weight shifts. "So who are you working for, and what exactly are you here to *do*?"

She's not hostile. Not yet. But she's stopped assuming you're on the same side.

Player: "I am merely a research assistant." I look at her for a second, then brush some dirt off my clothes. "I do have a secondary objective, which--seeing as how you're not on the crew manifest--I can assume is compatible with your own. To protect the company's ass in the event of a catastrophic failure."
Warden: Something shifts in Maren's posture — not relaxation exactly, but a recalibration. Her shoulders drop a half-inch. Her hand, which had been hovering near her jacket pocket, finds somewhere else to be.

"Protect the company's ass." She says it quietly, almost to herself. Then, with the careful tone of someone laying down a test: "And how exactly does a *research assistant* do that?"

"Because the thing that needs protecting — the specific thing — is sitting in the captain's terminal in Module 1. An encrypted comms log." She pauses, watching your face. Watching what your face does. "I've been trying to access it for three days. The encryption is above my clearance and the station's main systems are degraded. I need someone who can get past that."

She lets that hang between you.

"So. Research assistant." The lanyard swings slightly as she tilts her head. "What kind of secondary objectives are we talking about, exactly?"

Player: "That shouldn't be a problem," I say and stand. "The terminal is in module 1? Would you care to join me?" I start heading toward the door to module 1.
Warden: Maren doesn't move immediately. She watches you cross toward the door with the focused attention of someone running calculations.

Then she picks up a small bag from behind the cabinet — packed, you notice, already packed — and follows.

The walkway between Module 2 and Module 1 is a pressurized tube about four meters long, amber-lit, with the faint vibration of the station's attitude thrusters cycling underneath your feet. A condensation line has formed along the ceiling. The station groans once, low and structural, and then settles.

"How long have you been on the station?" she asks, watching the corridor ahead rather than you. Her voice is measured. Conversational in the way that isn't.

The captain's terminal is against the far wall. Its status light is a steady, patient amber.

[PLAYER INPUT]
"I arrived with the original crew, 14 months ago."I say. "You?" I enter module 1 and proceed to hack into the captain's terminal.

input.playerText:
Maren goes quiet for a moment.

"Fourteen months," she says, behind you. "Then you were here when it started."

Not an accusation. Something closer to the opposite — relief at finding someone with firsthand knowledge, mixed with the careful recalculation of what that means about what you've seen.

"I came in three weeks ago," she adds. "Supply audit cover. I've been trying to get into that terminal since day two."

Module 1 opens around you — the command module, stale and still. The captain's chair sits at a slight angle. Consoles in standby mode display slow amber pulses. The station shifts underfoot, a long low complaint from the spine, and then steadies.

The captain's terminal is against the far wall, its status light holding steady. You cross to it.

The interface responds to your touch, but the comms log is behind a multi-layer encryption package — Helix corporate standard, but heavily modified. Someone with elevated clearance ran a secondary lockout at some point after the incident. This is not a routine password.

Go ahead and make an **Intellect save** — roll 1d100, target 30. A success means you break through cleanly. A failure means you're in but it takes time you may not have, and the terminal logs the attempt.

proposedCanon.summary: Maren disclosed she arrived three weeks ago under supply audit cover and has been trying to access the terminal for three weeks.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 4, entering Module 1

proposedCanon.summary: LX-7 and Maren Voss entered Module 1 and LX-7 is now at the captain's terminal attempting to hack the encrypted comms log.
proposedCanon.context: Turn 4, Module 1 command bay

worldFacts.module_1_terminal_encryption: Captain's terminal comms log is protected by a modified Helix corporate multi-layer encryption package with a secondary lockout applied after the incident — above standard clearance level.

<world_facts>
module_2_description: Biological research bay. Overturned benches, shattered sample containers, dried dark biological stains on floor tiles. Chemical-organic smell. Emergency amber lighting.
module_1_description: Command module. Emergency lighting on, consoles in standby. Captain's chair empty and slightly turned. Captain's terminal against the far wall with a steady amber status light.
